# Forge Blueprint Template

This file defines the canonical YAML structure that the ForgeAI god-agent must produce when generating a blueprint. Copy this structure exactly, filling in values from the user's request and codebase scan.

---

## Blueprint Format

```yaml
# forge-blueprint.md
# Auto-generated by ForgeAI — DO NOT edit manually unless marked MANUAL OVERRIDE

project:
  name: string                    # e.g. "REST API with Auth"
  description: string             # one-sentence summary
  tech_stack: [string]            # e.g. [TypeScript, Next.js, Supabase, Zod]
  entry_point: string             # e.g. "src/index.ts"
  test_command: string            # e.g. "npm run test"
  typecheck_command: string       # e.g. "tsc --noEmit"
  lint_command: string            # e.g. "npx eslint src --ext .ts"

files:
  - path: "src/utils/hash.ts"     # relative from project root
    status: pending               # pending | coding | watching | done | escalated | skipped
    functions:
      - name: hashPassword
        params:
          - { name: password, type: string }
        returns: "Promise<string>"
        deps: []                  # list of "file:function" this function CALLS (not imports)
        description: "Hashes a plaintext password using bcrypt with salt rounds 12"
        complexity: simple        # simple | medium | complex → maps to model tier
        test:
          input: '"mypassword"'
          expected: 'string starting with "$2b$"'
        status: pending           # pending | coding | watching | done | escalated

  - path: "src/utils/hash.ts"
    status: pending
    functions:
      - name: verifyHash
        params:
          - { name: password, type: string }
          - { name: hash, type: string }
        returns: "Promise<boolean>"
        deps: []
        description: "Compares plaintext password against bcrypt hash"
        complexity: simple
        test:
          input: '"mypassword", "$2b$12$..."'
          expected: "true"
        status: pending

  - path: "src/db/users.ts"
    status: pending
    functions:
      - name: findUserByEmail
        params:
          - { name: email, type: string }
          - { name: db, type: DatabaseClient }
        returns: "Promise<User | null>"
        deps: ["src/types/user.ts:User"]
        description: "Fetches a single user row from the users table by email. Returns null if not found."
        complexity: medium
        test:
          input: '"existing@example.com", mockDb'
          expected: '{ id: "123", email: "existing@example.com", ... }'
        status: pending

  - path: "src/auth/login.ts"
    status: pending
    functions:
      - name: loginUser
        params:
          - { name: email, type: string }
          - { name: password, type: string }
          - { name: db, type: DatabaseClient }
        returns: "Promise<AuthResult>"
        deps:
          - "src/db/users.ts:findUserByEmail"
          - "src/utils/hash.ts:verifyHash"
          - "src/types/auth.ts:AuthResult"
        description: "Validates credentials against db, creates JWT session token, returns AuthResult"
        complexity: medium
        test:
          input: '"user@example.com", "correctpassword", mockDb'
          expected: '{ success: true, token: "<jwt>" }'
        status: pending

dependency_graph:
  # Key: file path. Value: list of files it depends on.
  # Leaf nodes (empty deps) build first.
  "src/types/user.ts": []
  "src/types/auth.ts": []
  "src/utils/hash.ts": []
  "src/db/users.ts": ["src/types/user.ts"]
  "src/auth/login.ts": ["src/db/users.ts", "src/utils/hash.ts", "src/types/auth.ts"]

build_order:
  # Ordered list of batches — each batch can run in PARALLEL
  # Functions within a batch have no interdependencies
  - batch: 1
    files: ["src/types/user.ts", "src/types/auth.ts", "src/utils/hash.ts"]
  - batch: 2
    files: ["src/db/users.ts"]
  - batch: 3
    files: ["src/auth/login.ts"]

metadata:
  generated_at: "ISO timestamp"
  forge_version: "1.0"
  total_functions: 5
  completed: 0
  escalated: 0
```

---

## Complexity → Model Mapping

| Complexity | Criteria | Model |
|---|---|---|
| `simple` | Pure functions, no I/O, no branching, <20 lines | `claude-haiku-4-5` |
| `medium` | DB calls, validation, moderate branching, 20–60 lines | `claude-sonnet-4-6` |
| `complex` | Multi-step pipelines, auth flows, >60 lines, security-critical | `claude-opus-4-6` |

---

## Rules for God-Agent Blueprint Generation

1. **Scan the codebase first** — use Glob to find existing files. Reuse existing types/utilities, don't recreate them.
2. **Every function gets its own entry** — one function per YAML block, never group.
3. **Deps are function-level, not file-level** — `"src/db/users.ts:findUserByEmail"` not `"src/db/users.ts"`.
4. **Leaf nodes first** — types, pure utils, and constants have empty `deps: []` and go in batch 1.
5. **Complexity is conservative** — when in doubt, classify one tier higher (better output, slightly more cost).
6. **Test cases are concrete** — use real-looking input/expected values, not `"some string"`.
7. **No circular deps** — if detected, flag in `metadata` and restructure.
8. **Status starts as `pending`** — god-agent updates status as agents complete.
